channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: Republican State Sen. David Argall asked the sixth grader if it was "appropriate to give pornographic magazines to kindergarteners.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: In private school voucher push, Gov. Abbott breaks through by playing hardball
item.description: Anti-voucher Republicans said they voted for the bill so they could fight to improve it, and also to fend off political retribution.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: AP and the Trump administration argue over presidential access before appeals judges
item.description: The Associated Press and Trump administration were back in a Washington court as part of a high-stakes battle over who can control which journalists can question the president.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump: Jimmy Carter died a happy man because Biden holds title for ‘worst’ president in history
item.description: President Donald Trump said the late President Jimmy Carter could die peacefully knowing he wasn’t the worst U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge grants detained U of M student bond but he‘ll stay in ICE custody as feds appeal
item.description: An international student facing deportation was granted bond by a judge earlier this week. However, he will stay locked up after the Department of Homeland Security filed an appeal.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear oral arguments in birthright citizenship case
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to hear arguments in May over President Donald Trump’s executive order aimed at ending birthright citizenship for children of noncitizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Pennsylvania Jews reminded of Tree of Life shooting after arson attack on Josh Shapiro's home
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A year ago, Carole Zawatsky was invited by Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro to a Passover Seder, held in the same room at the governor’s official residence that was devastated by an arsonist just hours after Shapiro hosted this year’s Jewish holiday feast.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, MARK SCOLFORO and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: At contentious Wisconsin elections meeting, Democratic chair threatens to oust Republican official
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin Elections Commission discussion of uncounted ballots in Madison devolved into a shouting match Thursday, with the Democratic chair threatening to kick a Republican commissioner out of the meeting.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican uses George Washington, American Revolution to reject notion Ukraine should surrender to Russia
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon pointed to the American Revolution to push back against the idea that Ukraine should surrender to Russia. Bacon has advocated for the U.S. and Europe to "arm Ukraine to the max.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr. should use troops to close fast-food joints, Tim Dillon jokes
item.description: Dillon said Robert F. Kennedy Jr. faces an uphill battle by challenging what the comedian calls the “junk food industry.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom says California will sue over Trump tariffs
item.description: “President Trump’s unlawful tariffs are wreaking chaos on California families, businesses, and our economy — driving up prices and threatening jobs," Newsom said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: White House invites Rachel Morin's mother to press briefing to share daughter's story
item.description: The mother of a Maryland woman who was killed by an individual who entered the country illegally addressed reporters at the White House on Wednesday as the Trump administration seeks to go on offense over the case of an individual who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel: Liberals 'viciously attacking comedians' are a 'big part of why Trump' won
item.description: "I think a lot of the outrage is completely manufactured, and it’s like, a lot of these people who are angry aren’t really angry," Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration says it will exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from ‘reciprocal’ tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tariffs could impact Tampa Bay shoppers, businesses by summer, economist says
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Cheri Hudson owns clothing store Imagine That!!! in South Tampa. It has been her business for 25 years. She is a supporter of President Trump’s trade policies. “I trust Donald Trump. It's only going to be a matter of time. The tariffs are fair.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Deadline arrives as Nevada lawmakers work furiously to pass bills out of committee
item.description: A deadline today will be the death knell for some of the nearly 1,100 bills in the Nevada Legislature. Some of the bills never went to a vote. Others were dead on arrival, identified by Gov. Joe Lombardo as bills he would veto.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
